title: ochna magdalenae
intro: Ochna magdalenae, also known as the Bird's Egg Plant, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Madagascar. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating digestive issues. Ochna magdalenae is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used to make a tincture or be applied topically as a poultice to treat various ailments.
